title: ModelEngine-Group/nexent
description: Nexent is a zero-code platform for auto-generating production-grade AI agents using Harness Engineering principles — unified tools, skills, memory, and orchestration with built-in constraints, feedback loops, and control planes.

title: ai-boost/awesome-harness-engineering
description: Awesome list for AI agent harness engineering: tools, patterns, evals, memory, MCP, permissions, observability, and orchestration.

title: bgdnvk/clanker
description: autonomous systems engineering cli agent for any cloud environment: AWS, GCP, Cloudflare, etc

title: Picrew/awesome-agent-harness
description: An awesome list of Agent Harness engineering resources, including GitHub projects, tools, benchmarks, and practical guides.

title: bytedance/deer-flow
description: An open-source long-horizon SuperAgent harness that researches, codes, and creates. With the help of sandboxes, memories, tools, skill, subagents and message gateway, it handles different levels of tasks that could take minutes to hours.

title: can1357/oh-my-pi
description: ⌥  AI Coding agent for the terminal — hash-anchored edits, optimized tool harness, LSP, Python, browser, subagents, and more

title: rohitg00/agentmemory
description: #1 Persistent memory for AI coding agents based on real-world benchmarks

title: zilliztech/memsearch
description: A persistent, unified memory layer for all your AI agents (e.g. Claude Code, Codex), backed by Markdown and Milvus.

title: study8677/antigravity-workspace-template
description: Workspace template + MCP server for Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cursor & Windsurf. Multi-agent knowledge engine (ag-refresh / ag-ask) that turns any codebase into a queryable AI assistant.

title: agent-sh/agnix
description: The missing linter and lsp for AI coding assistants. Validate CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, hooks, MCP. Plugin for all major IDEs included, with autofixes.

On the Footprints of Reviewer Bots Feedback on Agentic Pull Requests in OSS GitHub Repositories
description: Autonomous coding agents are reshaping software development by creating pull requests (PRs) on GitHub, referred to as agentic PRs. In parallel, the review process is also becoming autonomous, thereby making reviewer bots key actors in the assessment of these agentic PRs. However, their influence on PR acceptance and resolution remains unclear. This study empirically investigates the relationship b
authors: Syeda Kaneez Fatima, Yousuf Abrar, Abdul Rehman Tahir

title: Agentic Witnessing: Pragmatic and Scalable TEE-Enabled Privacy-Preserving Auditing
description: Auditing the semantic properties of proprietary data creates a fundamental tension: verification requires transparent access, while proprietary rights demand confidentiality. While Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) ensure privacy, they are typically limited to precise algebraic constraints and are ill-suited for verifying qualitative, unstructured properties, such as the logic within a codebase.
